title: Set 03 - SSL Deprecation, TLS Migration, Certificates & Hardening
subject: Cyber Security
topic: SSL Deprecation, TLS Migration, Certificates & Hardening

questions.question: Which protocol replaced SSL for secure web communication?
questions.description: TLS replaced SSL due to security weaknesses in older SSL versions.

questions.question: Why is SSL 3.0 considered unsafe?
options: It is vulnerable to attacks such as POODLE
It uses too much disk space
questions.description: SSL 3.0 has known design weaknesses and should be disabled.

questions.question: Which term is often used casually but imprecisely for TLS certificates?
questions.description: Many people say SSL certificate even when the modern protocol is TLS.

questions.question: What should be done with SSLv2 and SSLv3 on production servers?
options: Use them only for admin users
questions.description: Legacy SSL versions should be disabled because they are insecure.
